text:
sun and such other portents on the new-Moon and full-Moon days
and also at the Sun's entrance into a new Sign of the Zodiac
every month, one should predict fluctuations in the prices of com-
modities. These portents observed on other days forebode sufferings
to kings through wars or bloodshed.
[There are many technical terms in this verse. For TJlka,
Danda, Parivesa and Paridhi see ch. XXXIII-8; XXX-16; XXXIV-
1 and XXXVII-1 respectively. From this verse it is clear that the
prospects of prices have to be determined thiough the phenomena
occurring on the three critical days viz. new Moon, full Moon and
the Sun's entry into a new Sign of the Zodiac, In fact this chapter
and the previous two are to be read together for a complete under-
standing of the prospects for crops and their prices. We find the
same sage Kasyapa being quoted as authority on this subject.
Cf. SFTOTT—
